title: WATCH: Romans 13 Teaches That Civil Government Must Submit to Christ
author: Rachel Burkey
orientation: Rachel Burkey flips the conventional reading of Romans 13 on its head, arguing the chapter is not merely a chain to bind Christians to obedience but a charter that obligates civil authorities themselves to study, obey, and answer to God. The piece presses the church to remind politicians of their duty as God's servants, especially in light of legalized abortion.
audiences: pro-lifers who appeal to Romans 13 for incrementalism

questions.question: What is the typical use of Romans 13 that this article seeks to invert?
questions.answer: Romans 13 is most often wielded as a one-way leash on Christians, demanding their submission to civil rulers, while the corresponding obligation that those same rulers must answer to God is left unpreached.
questions.quote: Romans 13 is commonly used to teach Christians about our duty to obey civil authorities. But rarely, if ever, is this chapter used to teach what the Lord reveals concerning the duty of those authorities themselves.

questions.question: On what basis does Burkey argue that civil rulers are accountable to God rather than autonomous?
questions.answer: Because all authority is delegated by God, civil magistrates are God's servants in the civil sphere, which means they are not free agents and must study and obey His Word or face His judgment.
questions.quote: All governing authorities, being delegated and appointed by God, are *His servants* and are accountable to *Him*. As such, they have a duty to study and obey God’s Word — or face the consequences.

questions.question: What classical Protestant voice does the article enlist to rebuke modern legislators?
questions.answer: She quotes the 17th century Puritan Richard Baxter, who told the rulers of his day that civil law must be subordinate to God's law and that no man can be a competent legislator without competent theology.
questions.quote: Remember that all your laws are to subservient to the laws of God, and never contrary to them, nor independent of them… A man can be a good theologian that is no [legislator], but he can be no good [legislator] that understandeth not theology.

questions.question: What punishment does Jesus warn unfaithful overseers to expect, and how does Burkey apply that warning to politicians?
questions.answer: Jesus warns that servants entrusted with authority over others who abuse those in their care will be cut to pieces, and Burkey asks why corrupt politicians are never confronted with that very threat.
questions.quote: When is the last time a corrupt, ungodly politician was told to submit to Christ lest Christ cut him into pieces?

questions.question: Who, according to Burkey, gets to define good and evil for the civil magistrate?
questions.answer: Because rulers are servants and not masters, they have no authority to define morality themselves; the master, God, defines good and evil and hands those definitions down through His Word.
questions.quote: (Good and evil are, of course, defined by the master and not the servant.)

questions.question: How does the article quantify the failure of American civil authorities by their own God-given mandate?
questions.answer: She points to over sixty million children sacrificed nationally, nearly a million added every year, and 5,000 murdered annually even in the supposedly pro-life state of Oklahoma, all under the watch of magistrates God instituted to uphold justice.
questions.quote: More than sixty million children have been sacrificed in our country. Almost a million image bearers are added to that total nationally every year, and 5,000 are murdered annually in pro-life Oklahoma.

questions.question: Whose responsibility is it to confront civil rulers with their duty before God?
questions.answer: Burkey lays the burden squarely on the church, framing the question rhetorically so that no other institution can plausibly fill the role the bride of Christ is abdicating.
questions.quote: Who is going to remind our civil authorities of their duty before God if the bride of Christ will not?

questions.question: What concrete commitments does the article ask Christians to resolve to make?
questions.answer: Christians are to learn what God commands of magistrates, speak Scripture to them, hold them accountable, lovingly warn them, repent of their own apathy toward abortion, and speak up for the pre-born by faith.
questions.quote: Hold Godʼs civil servants accountable to God’s commands

questions.question: What does Burkey say a Christian must repent of if they have remained silent toward unfaithful magistrates?
questions.answer: Believers must confess and repent before God of any participation in or apathy toward the abortion holocaust that has been allowed to continue because the church refused to hold civil servants accountable.
questions.quote: Confess to God and repent of any participation in or apathy toward the abortion holocaust that we have allowed to take place by not holding Godʼs civil servants accountable

related.title: Does Romans 13 Really Instruct Christians To Allow the Killing of Babies?
related.reason: Directly companion article on the same Pauline text, attacking the misuse of Romans 13 to justify Christian passivity toward state-sanctioned abortion.

related.title: A Mother Is a Magistrate: Why Duress Is No Defense for Abortion
related.reason: Extends the doctrine of God-delegated authority from civil rulers to parents, reinforcing that all magistrates answer to God's law.

related.title: C.R. Cali: How to Exhort Legislators Who Support Abolition and Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Practical follow-through on the article's call to speak Scripture to civil servants and hold them accountable.

related.title: Refuting Your Legislators' Excuses for Not Abolishing Abortion
related.reason: Equips activists to confront the very magistrates Burkey says must be reminded of their duty before God.

related.title: Christian, Not Pro-Life
related.reason: Presses the same theological priority Burkey assumes: Christ's lordship over civic engagement supersedes secular pro-life framing.

related.title: On Repentance, Abolitionists Must Lead by Example: An Open Letter
related.reason: Develops the article's closing call for Christians to confess their own apathy toward the abortion holocaust before demanding repentance from magistrates.

related.title: SBC President Bart Barber Gives a Lesson in How Not to Repent
related.reason: Shows what failed accountability looks like in practice when church leaders model the very evasions Burkey warns against.
